hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance SSTR2 selectivity by forming complementary interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target binding by avoiding off-target-conserved electrostatic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis is updated to prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) to disrupt off-target interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5. This will help increase Δmargin and achieve SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions allows us to design mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.

Introducing charged or polar residues at these positions can disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore. These mutations are designed to reduce off-target affinity while preserving SSTR2 binding.
